hero.subtitle: International migration, cross-border legal strategy, and Argentine legal representation — all in one place.
hero.subtitleAlt: Comprehensive legal support for individuals, families, and businesses navigating international transitions.
hero.servicesHint: Click and scroll down to explore

whoWeAre.title: One team, one shared vision
whoWeAre.paragraph2: Having personally experienced the process of living and navigating legal systems abroad, one of us understands firsthand the uncertainty, challenges, and emotions that often come with building a life in another country. Her practice focuses on international migration, global mobility, and cross-border legal matters.
whoWeAre.paragraph3: The other, after graduating with honors, has developed her career across both the public and private legal sectors in Argentina, including experience within the Superior Provincial Court of Córdoba as a relatora, contributing directly to the drafting and development of judicial decisions. Her background provides the strong domestic legal and litigation expertise often essential in complex international matters.

sisters.name: I. Galli
sisters.summary: Attorney with a background in civil, business, and family law.
sisters.bio: I graduated from the School of Law at the National University of Córdoba at the age of 23, receiving recognition as an outstanding graduate and earning a place on the Law School's Honor Roll. Currently pursuing postgraduate studies, convinced that continuous learning is an essential part of legal practice.
sisters.alt: Portrait of I. Galli, attorney at Galli's & Co.

sisters.name: Victoria Galli
sisters.summary: Argentinian lawyer specialized in international mobility.
sisters.bio: Graduated from the School of Law at the National University of Córdoba at the age of 23, with a specialization in International Mobility and a focus on Argentine, Italian, Spanish, and other immigration and citizenship frameworks. As a proud dual citizen of Argentina and Italy, I bring both personal and professional experience to the firm.
sisters.alt: Portrait of Victoria Galli, attorney at Galli's & Co.

whoWeAre.networkText: Our interdisciplinary approach allows us to work alongside a trusted network of specialized legal professionals in Argentina  including Tax, labor, Real Estate, Criminal and corporate law practitioners, as well as partner law firms across Europe and the United States. This collaborative structure enables coordinated, well rounded legal support across multiple jurisdictions and areas of law.
whoWeAre.globalReachText: Argentina, Europe and the United States working as one coordinated network.

ofCounsel.name: Mario Tedesco
ofCounsel.bio1: He's a proud Neapolitan (Italian) licensed lawyer, with more than thirty years of legal experience focused on Italian citizenship Iure Sanguinis.
ofCounsel.bio2: He speaks English, Italian, and Spanish and he personally handles these matters representing clients in courts across Italy.

ofCounsel.name: Gian Ignacio Rondi
ofCounsel.bio2: He has spent over 3 years working within the Judicial Branch of the Province of Córdoba and the Public Prosecutor's Office in criminal investigation. He specializes in labor law, workplace accidents, occupational diseases, and criminal defense.

ofCounsel.name: Federico Teliczan
ofCounsel.role: Civil, Business, and Family Law Specialist.
ofCounsel.bio1: Federico Teliczan is an attorney practicing law in the provinces of Córdoba and La Pampa.
ofCounsel.bio2: His practice focuses on Civil, Commercial, Family, and Estate Law. He is a Founding Partner of the T&P Law Firm and a Partner at Martínez Paz & Asociados. His practice encompasses the judicial and extrajudicial representation of individuals and corporations, drafting legal pleadings and lawsuits, structuring contracts and agreements, and providing comprehensive legal counsel in civil, commercial, and family matters.

hero.title: International Mobility to Argentina
hero.description: Securing your legal status in Argentina should not feel like a bureaucratic maze. We guide clients from the RaDEX platform and document legalizations through filings, strategy and daily follow-up, so they can focus on their new life, investment or career.

intro.title: International mobility to Argentina is a legal strategy, not just a move.
intro.lead: From the cultural depth of Buenos Aires to the landscapes of Mendoza and Patagonia, Argentina attracts people, families and companies looking for a meaningful place to build. We manage the route end to end — intake, risk review, legalizations, RaDEX workflow, filings and daily monitoring — until the file reaches the result it needs, under current rules including DNU 366/2025.

highlights.title: The legal lens
highlights.text: Dossier structure, evidence thresholds and pathway selection settled before anything is filed.

highlights.title: The operational lens
highlights.text: RaDEX setup, legalizations and daily monitoring until the DNI is in hand.

sectionsHeading.title: Every route to legal status, coordinated in a single file.
sectionsHeading.subtitle: From temporary residency to citizenship — we map the pathway you can actually sustain under current immigration rules.

sections.title: Temporary Residency
sections.description: Usually the first step: renewable legal status — typically one year, in some cases up to three — plus a temporary Argentine DNI.
items: Rentista — verified passive income or investment returns generated outside Argentina.
Pensioner — a steady official pension from a foreign government or international organization.
Migrant Worker — a registered corporate contract or formal job offer with an Argentine employer.
Investor — a productive local investment or project of national interest, reviewed against the current threshold.
Student, Scientists & Specialized Staff — enrollment or specialized technical contracts with recognized institutions.
Athletes & Artists — creatives and athletes officially contracted by local institutions.
MERCOSUR Citizens — a simplified fast-track residence for member and associate state nationals.
Medical Treatment & Humanitarian Grounds — coverage for patients, families and protected persons.

sections.title: Permanent Residency
sections.description: An indefinite status for those establishing roots in Argentina, with a lifelong unrestricted DNI.
items: Family Ties — spouse, parent or child of a native or naturalized Argentine, or of a permanent resident.
Children of Argentines — born abroad to Argentine parents, by operation of law where applicable.
Arraigo (Track Record) — after continuous lawful temporary residence: two years MERCOSUR, three years non-MERCOSUR.
Diplomats — former foreign officials who concluded their duties and met the local track record.
Refugees — individuals recognized by CONARE who met the required residency timelines.

sections.title: Argentine Citizenship
sections.description: Citizenship is handled as a separate strategic path, with documentary rigor and legal timing aligned to the route you can actually support.
items: Naturalization assessed through lawful residence, identity proof and civil-status records.
Investment-led strategies reviewed for source of funds and traceability under current criteria.
A documentary file built to sustain the petition before the competent authority.

sections.title: The legal filter before anything is filed
sections.description: We do not just assemble paperwork. We audit the file first — the legal route, the immigration basis, the document chain, the income evidence, the platform workflow and the fallback strategy if the case becomes contested.
items: Document legalizations, apostilles and certified translations.
Income-source review and SMVM-based threshold checks.

feature.quote: The move feels fluid because the file is controlled — every threshold mapped before a single document is submitted.
feature.author: Galli's & Co. — International Mobility

closing.title: Ready to secure your Argentine status?
closing.subtitle: Book a strategy session and we will map the cleanest route for your case.

hero.title: U.S. Visa Guidance & Immigration Pathways
hero.description: The United States offers a wide range of temporary and permanent options. We help individuals, families, entrepreneurs and professionals find the right pathway and connect with trusted U.S. immigration attorneys.

intro.title: Understanding which pathway fits your goals is the first and most important step.
intro.lead: Our founder previously worked with U.S. immigration law firms across visitor visas, family-based immigration, naturalization, investor and employment-based pathways. That experience lets us identify the right strategy and connect you with experienced counsel.

highlights.title: Practical experience
highlights.text: Hands-on understanding of the consular and USCIS systems.

highlights.title: Trusted network
highlights.text: Carefully selected U.S. immigration attorneys matched to your case.

sectionsHeading.title: From a short visit to long-term residency — mapped to your objectives.
sectionsHeading.subtitle: Each pathway is explained with the scope we cover and the legal partners we coordinate.

sections.title: B-1/B-2 Visitor Visa Guidance
sections.description: End-to-end support for tourism and business visitor applications, including difficult histories.

sections.title: Business & Investment Pathways
sections.description: Options for entrepreneurs and investors planning to operate in or relocate to the United States.

sections.title: Family-Based Immigration & Citizenship
sections.description: Reuniting families and securing long-term status through family ties and naturalization.

sections.title: Employment-Based Pathways
sections.description: Routes for skilled and professional workers seeking sponsored, long-term residency.

sections.title: Additional Immigration Matters
sections.description: The procedural and record-related work that keeps a case on track.

feature.quote: Every immigration case is unique — our role is to clarify your options and connect you with the right legal team to move forward.

closing.title: Ready to understand your U.S. options?

hero.title: European Pathways & Italian Citizenship
hero.description: Among Europe's citizenship routes, Italian citizenship remains one of the most sought-after for those with Italian ancestry. We guide families through recognition, registration and relocation across the EU.

intro.title: Italian citizenship is more than heritage — it is access to the rights of EU membership.
intro.lead: Through our partnership with Italian citizenship attorney Mario Tedesco and his specialized team in Italy, we provide end-to-end support — from eligibility analysis and document reconstruction to recognition proceedings worldwide. Recent reforms mean choosing the right pathway matters more than ever.

highlights.title: Italian legal team
highlights.text: Direct coordination with counsel in Italy on every recognition proceeding.

highlights.title: Case-by-case strategy
highlights.text: The right route based on ancestry, family circumstances and current law.

sectionsHeading.title: Every route to recognition, registration and life in Europe.
sectionsHeading.subtitle: Eligibility and immigration implications vary — each pathway is assessed individually.

sections.title: Judicial Recognition of Italian Citizenship
sections.description: Court-based recognition in Italy, ideal for maternal-line and reform-affected cases, with the possibility of residing while the case proceeds.
items: Cases where consular recognition is unavailable.
Families seeking a centralized, court-led process.

sections.title: Recognition Through Residency in Italy
sections.description: Establishing residency in Italy to pursue recognition directly before the local comune.

sections.title: Consular Recognition of Jure Sanguinis
sections.description: Recognition through the competent Italian consulate based on your jurisdiction of residence.

sections.title: Registration of Minor Children
sections.description: Time-sensitive registration following the March 2025 reform, with transitional windows running through 2029.
items: Consular filings and Fast It setup.

sections.title: AIRE Registration & Consular Compliance
sections.description: Keeping your Italian records accurate and your consular standing in order.
items: Fast It management and consular compliance.

sections.title: European Relocation & Establishment
sections.description: Practical support to settle and establish yourself once your European status is secured.

feature.quote: Recent reforms have introduced time-sensitive opportunities — early action can preserve your family's eligibility.
feature.author: Galli's & Co. — European Pathways

closing.title: Ready to explore your European pathway?
closing.subtitle: Tell us about your ancestry and goals, and we will identify the route that fits your case.
